commits.subject: fix(cssColor): three bugs branch findings — opaque-black sentinel, alpha-zero probe, probe side-effect
commits.body:
Bugs-branch trigger pass against the branch diff found three theoretical
issues worth hardening. None observed in production yet, all cheap to
preempt.

1. **SENTINEL_RE over-matched opaque black.** The regex marked the
   alpha component optional, so `rgb(0, 0, 0)` (opaque black) would
   have matched the sentinel had any browser ever serialized opaque
   #000000 as a 3-component form. Per spec, opaque colors serialize to
   #RRGGBB — but a non-conforming browser or one deviating during CSS
   Color 4 transition could emit `rgb(0, 0, 0)`, and opaque black
   would then be mis-classified as rejected → caller gets null → falls
   back to hex. On graphiki today the dark-theme `base-content` is
   near-white, so the real impact is zero, but the over-match is a
   latent bug. Fix: require four zero components in the regex.

2. **Integrity probe didn't validate alpha.** The probe checked R/G/B
   channels of the #ff0000 and #0000ff readbacks but not alpha. A
   hypothetical hardened environment that returned [255, 0, 0, 0] and
   [0, 0, 255, 0] would pass the probe (RGB distinct, in ballpark) but
   every subsequent rasterization would return alpha=0, so every
   Cytoscape element would render fully transparent with no warning.
   Fix: add `red[3] === 255 && blue[3] === 255` to the probe's success
   condition.

3. **Probe left blue pixel in canvas.** The probe ends with
   `fillStyle = '#0000ff'` and pixel (0,0) painted blue. Stage 2's
   clearRect + fillRect overwrites this before reading, so no bug
   today. But a future optimization that removes the clearRect call
   ("we're about to fillRect anyway") would silently leak the probe's
   blue pixel into the first normalization result. Fix: call
   `clearRect` in a `finally` block at the end of the probe so the
   canvas is left in a clean state regardless of probe outcome.

Tests:
- New test: opaque black round-trips through rasterization even when
  the mock fillStyle getter returns `rgb(0, 0, 0)` — catches SENTINEL_RE
  regression.
- New test: probe with zeroed-alpha getImageData returns null instead
  of trusting the canvas — catches the alpha-blind probe.

All 347 tests pass. Lint clean, typecheck clean.

Also: removed `Confirm DaisyUI pastel token → oklch value mapping` from
docs/TODO.md. Investigation revealed the tokens were in the dark theme
(not pastel — report was from dark mode), confirming the mapping to
--color-warning / info / success / secondary / accent is correct.

commits.subject: harden(cssColor): fingerprint-protection probe, CSS Color 5 catch-all, non-tautological tripwire
commits.body:
Risk-pass follow-up to the cssColor rasterization fix (af48633 +
9991eda). Addresses forward-looking issues that the green test suite
couldn't catch because the existing mock-canvas tests encoded the
same assumptions the implementation depended on.

Code — cssColor.ts:
- **Canvas integrity probe.** On first real use, paint known-distinct
  colors (#ff0000 then #0000ff) and read each back via getImageData.
  If reads collapse to the same RGBA (Firefox
  `privacy.resistFingerprinting`, Tor Browser, some hardened
  anti-fingerprinting extensions) or the probe throws,
  `canvasIntegrityOK` flips to false and every subsequent
  `normalizeColor` returns null — callers fall back to their hex
  literal (visible + correct) instead of a silently-wrong rasterized
  value. Probe cached once per canvas lifetime; reset by
  `clearCSSColorCache`. Gated on `fillRect`/`getImageData` existing so
  test mocks without rasterization support are not mis-classified.
- **Harden SENTINEL_RE.** Previous regex only accepted
  `rgba(0, 0, 0, 0)` (comma-separated, any whitespace). New regex
  also accepts `rgb(0 0 0 / 0)` and `rgba(0 0 0 / 0)` (CSS Color 4
  slash-alpha syntax). Prevents a browser that emits the new form
  from misclassifying the sentinel check and flagging every assignment
  as rejected.
- **Extend rejection guards.** Split the previous
  `MODERN_SYNTAX_RE` into two layers: (a) a hit-list of known-rejected
  functions (`oklch`/`oklab`/`color`/`color-mix`/`lab`/`lch`/`hwb`/
  `light-dark`/`contrast-color`) and (b) an `UNKNOWN_FN_RE` catch-all
  with `KNOWN_SAFE_FN_RE` whitelist (`rgb`/`rgba`/`hsl`/`hsla`). Any
  future CSS Color 5+ function we haven't enumerated is rejected by
  the catch-all rather than silently leaking to Cytoscape.

Tests:
- **Upgrade `cytoscapeStyles.safety.test.ts` to a raster-aware mock.**
  The previous mock scripted `fillStyle` to downsample oklch to hex —
  same tautological pattern documented in AI_MISTAKES. The upgraded
  mock preserves raw values on `fillStyle` (simulates modern Chromium
  CSS Color 4 serialization) and returns RGBA bytes from `getImageData`
  derived from the expected-output hex. The tripwire now actually
  exercises the production rasterization path instead of a rigged
  fallback. Also expanded `UNKNOWN_FN_RE` coverage in the stylesheet
  walk assertion.
- **New cssColor tests:**
  - TRIPWIRE for `color-mix()` + UNKNOWN_FN catch-all via selective
    getImageData failure (probe passes on sRGB colors, fails on CSS
    Color 4 — simulates an extension that whitelists legacy syntax).
  - Positive + negative tests for the integrity probe (collapsed
    uniform readbacks return null; cooperative canvas returns hex).
  - Renamed the pre-existing "normalizeColor" describe block to
    "normalizeColor (fallback path — no getImageData)" so a filtered
    run surfaces both the fallback block and the modern-canvas block.
- Helper `installModernCanvas` auto-seeds probe colors in both the
  serialization and raster rule maps so individual tests don't have
  to remember.
- Replaced the older "tolerates rasterization failure when fillStyle
  already downsampled to hex" test with "returns null when
  getImageData throws during the integrity probe" — the new semantic
  (fail-closed when canvas can't rasterize) is strictly safer than
  the old (trust legacy fillStyle serialization on a misbehaving
  canvas).

Other:
- GraphCanvas.tsx — updated the `document.fonts.ready` comment block
  to clarify the re-apply's primary purpose (DaisyUI stylesheet not
  yet painted on Cytoscape init) is a lifecycle issue distinct from
  normalization. Rasterization is deterministic per-input; the
  re-apply still matters because CSS vars are initially unset.
- CLAUDE.md + docs/CODEBASE_DOCUMENTATION.md — reflected the new
  probe, the regex split, and the hardened sentinel coverage.
- docs/USER_ACTIONS.md — first entry added: merge
  `claude/review-claude-md-wuSD2` to `main` so Vercel deploys the
  fix. Production still serves the pre-fix build; re-test on Chrome
  147 Android after merge.

Tests: 342 -> 345 (3 new in cssColor). Lint clean, typecheck clean.

commits.subject: fix(cssColor): rasterize via getImageData to survive CSS Color 4 fillStyle preservation
commits.body:
Modern Chromium (Chrome 147 on Android, confirmed via production debug
report) implements CSS Color 4 serialization on canvas 2D fillStyle.
Setting `ctx.fillStyle = 'oklch(...)'` and reading `String(ctx.fillStyle)`
now round-trips the oklch value unchanged instead of downsampling to
legacy hex/rgba. The previous normalizer's central assumption — "the
HTML spec guarantees fillStyle serializes any valid CSS color to
#rrggbb / rgba(...) / #rrggbbaa" — is no longer true. Every DaisyUI
oklch token leaked through getCSSColor to Cytoscape, which rejected
them silently, breaking node:selected borders, dual-query A/B/bridge
colors, analysis highlights, and arc fallbacks.

The previous mock-canvas test suite could not catch this because it
replaced real browser behavior with explicit input→output rules. Tests
asserted "oklch → #eab308" because the mock was told to do that, not
because real canvases do it.

Fix:
- Replace fillStyle-serialization as the normalization primitive with
  rasterization: paint a 1x1 pixel via fillRect, read the 8-bit sRGB
  bytes via getImageData, serialize to #rrggbb (a=255) or rgba(...).
  Pixel readback is guaranteed sRGB 8-bit regardless of how fillStyle
  chooses to serialize, so CSS Color 4 preservation cannot leak.
- Keep sentinel-based rejection detection as stage 1 — fillStyle still
  ignores unparseable inputs, and catching that lets us return null so
  getCSSColor routes to its hex fallback.
- Add MODERN_SYNTAX_RE defense-in-depth: if getImageData throws
  (tainted canvas, CSP, privacy mode), any serialized value starting
  with oklch(/oklab(/color(/lab(/lch(/hwb( is treated as rejection
  rather than fed to Cytoscape.
- Update decision-doc header to reflect the real mechanism and add an
  "alternatives considered" entry rejecting fillStyle-downsample
  specifically so the assumption can't reappear silently.

Tests:
- New describe block "modern canvas fillStyle (CSS Color 4
  serialization)" with a raster-aware mock that simulates Chrome 147+
  behavior (fillStyle getter preserves oklch; getImageData returns the
  known sRGB tuple). Verifies normalizeColor returns hex, not oklch.
- New TRIPWIRE test asserts normalizeColor never returns a string
  starting with oklch(/oklab(/color(/lab(/lch(/hwb( for non-null
  outputs — catches any future regression at the unit-test layer
  instead of production.
- New test for rasterization failure + modern-syntax guard path.
- New test for rasterization failure + legacy hex path (older
  Chromium / WebKit) still caches successfully.
- All 21 existing tests pass unchanged — they exercise the fallback
  path where the mock ctx has no fillRect/getImageData methods, the
  try/catch catches the TypeError, and the serialized hex is returned.

Docs:
- Updated CLAUDE.md "Cytoscape color normalization" convention to
  describe the two-stage mechanism (sentinel rejection + raster
  normalize), note that modern Chromium preserves CSS Color 4 syntaxes
  on fillStyle, and reference the new TRIPWIRE test by name.

commits.body:
The old file crossed the 500-line "pause and consider" threshold in
CLAUDE.md. Each concern in the query pipeline is self-contained and
mostly imports from the shared AST types, so the split is mechanical.

New layout:
- src/services/query/ast.ts (39 lines) — AST types, CompareOp,
  GraphContext. Sole source of the query data model.
- src/services/query/tokenizer.ts (73 lines) — Token type + tokenize().
  Lexer for the text query language (label:X, OR, NOT, IN/OUT/CONN,
  paren grouping).
- src/services/query/parser.ts (199 lines) — Recursive-descent Parser
  class + parseQuery() public entry point. Tokenizer is internal;
  parseQuery wraps tokenize + parse.
- src/services/query/evaluator.ts (117 lines) — evaluateNode +
  compareValues + applyOp. Date→number→string coercion lives here
  because it's evaluator-specific, not a shape definition.
- src/services/query/builder.ts (132 lines) — BuilderFilter/BuilderStep
  types + builderFilterToAST / builderStepToAST. Converts the
  interactive query builder's structured output directly to AST
  without round-tripping through text.

src/services/queryParser.ts (22 lines) — Barrel re-export so
`import { ... } from './queryParser'` still works. queryEngine.ts
and its consumers don't need to change. Kept the filename as a
searchable landmark rather than forcing 20+ import-site edits.

commits.subject: test(graph): oklch-rejection tripwire for Cytoscape stylesheet output
commits.body:
Closes the Console-warning-budget TODO item. Defensive test specifically
for the class of bug that started this branch: DaisyUI's oklch() values
reaching Cytoscape's color parser and getting silently dropped.

src/components/graph/cytoscapeStyles.safety.test.ts (new, 6 cases)
- Seeds document.documentElement with realistic DaisyUI oklch CSS vars
  for the 7 tokens the stylesheet reads (warning/info/success/
  secondary/accent/primary/base-content).
- Installs a canvas mock that simulates the browser's oklch→hex
  normalization for each seeded value.
- Calls getCytoscapeStylesheet() for both 'bezier' and 'taxi' edge-
  curve styles. Walks every string-valued `*color*` property in the
  output (skipping Cytoscape `data(...)` mapper references — those
  resolve to values produced by getNodeColor/getArcColor, which have
  their own direct tests).
- Asserts each value matches /^(#[0-9a-f]{3,8}|rgba?\(.+\)|hsla?\(.+\)|
  transparent|[a-z]+)$/i AND does not start with any of oklch(, lch(,
  oklab(, lab(, color(, color-mix(.
- Separately exercises getNodeColor (labelless path via --color-primary)
  and getArcColor (titled path + untitled fallback).
- Uses a TS-safe narrow (`'style' in block`) for Cytoscape's
  StylesheetJsonBlock union (StylesheetStyle | StylesheetCSS).

Verified the tripwire trips: temporarily replaced the normalizeColor()
call in getCSSColor with a raw pass-through — 3 of 6 cases failed
loudly with messages like "node:selected { border-color: oklch(82% .189
84.429) } — starts with unparseable 'oklch('". Restored the fix before
committing.

What this catches:
- Direct regression: anyone removing/bypassing cssColor normalization
- New style property added with a literal oklch/color-mix/lch color
- PALETTE hex replaced with an oklch literal
- New CSS Color 4 function that Cytoscape doesn't know about

336 tests pass (up from 330). Lint + build clean.

Docs
- docs/TODO.md: console-warning-budget item removed (done).
- docs/SESSION_NOTES.md: new section describing the tripwire.

commits.subject: feat(debug): worker-side console interception forwards to DebugPill
commits.body:
Workers run @huggingface/transformers (ONNX Runtime + tokenizers). Any
console.warn / console.error they emit never reached DebugPill because
debugLog's console interception only patches the main thread. Same
class of blind spot as the oklch Cytoscape rejection — third-party
library warns into the void, nobody notices until a user reports a bug.

workers/workerDebugLog.ts (new)
- patchWorkerConsole() wraps self.console.warn/error in the worker
  global. Each call sends a typed envelope
  `{ type: 'debug', severity, event, details }` via postMessage before
  calling the original console method (so DevTools still shows the log).
- Reentrancy guard prevents infinite recursion if postMessage itself
  warns (e.g., from a polyfill).
- postMessage throws are swallowed so a debug-infrastructure bug can't
  crash the worker.
- Bails out when window is defined (main thread) or postMessage is
  unavailable — makes the module safe to import in tests without
  mutating the runner's console.
- isWorkerDebugEnvelope() type guard keeps the bridge dispatch sites
  type-safe.
- No auto-patch-on-import; workers call patchWorkerConsole() explicitly
  so tests have deterministic behavior.

workers/embeddingWorker.ts + tagSuggestionWorker.ts
- Each imports and calls patchWorkerConsole() at top-level, before
  anything else, so transformers.js warnings emitted during module
  load get forwarded.

services/embeddingWorkerBridge.ts + tagSuggestionService.ts
- handleWorkerMessage checks isWorkerDebugEnvelope FIRST (before init
  events), because transformers.js can warn during the init path.
  Matching envelopes dispatch to debugLog with source 'embedding' or
  'tagSuggestion' and the original severity.

workers/workerDebugLog.test.ts (new, 15 cases)
- Main-thread bail-out, postMessage-missing bail-out, warn/error
  forwarding, original-console passthrough, idempotency, argument
  coercion via String(), postMessage-throws-swallowed, reentrancy
  guard, plus isWorkerDebugEnvelope type-guard coverage (null/undef/
  wrong-type/unknown-severity/missing-event rejection).

  reflect worker-side interception and 500-entry cap.

commits.subject: chore(graph): bound cssColor cache, document fallback-call asymmetry, cover sentinel-caching
commits.body:
Three remaining items from the review sweep:

cssColor.ts (#12)
- MAX_CACHE_ENTRIES = 256 with FIFO eviction via a small cacheSet()
  helper. Practical workload is ~14 tokens × a handful of theme combos,
  so the cap is a defense against a future caller feeding unique strings
  per call (e.g., per-node computed colors) rather than a hot-path
  concern. FIFO (oldest-insertion wins eviction) beats LRU here because
  LRU needs delete-on-hit, defeating the cache's purpose.

cssColor.test.ts (#12, #18)
- New test "evicts oldest entry (FIFO) when the cache exceeds
  MAX_CACHE_ENTRIES": fills beyond the cap with unique inputs, mutates
  the shared rules Map in place (getCtx memoizes the first ctx, so a
  second installFakeCanvas wouldn't rebind), verifies color-0 evicted
  and color-259 still cached.
- New test "caches the sentinel itself when passed as raw input": the
  rejection check's `!isSentinel(raw)` short-circuit must let the
  sentinel value through to the cache path — otherwise a caller
  legitimately passing transparent-black pays the canvas cost every
  time.

GraphCanvas.tsx (#15)
- Inline comment documenting why the two getCSSColor calls live here
  rather than being pulled into cytoscapeStyles.ts: they serve per-
  element fallbacks (node without label, arc without title) on the
  data-sync path, not stylesheet-wide colors on the theme-apply path.
  Different lifecycles, different concerns — moving them would couple
  them to the wrong trigger.

Everything else from the review (item #11 zero-alpha forms, item #17
doc callsite count) is either already documented as a known limitation
or was an agent miscount. No code change needed for those.

commits.subject: fix(graph): harden cssColor rejection path; restore wheelSensitivity via config; fonts.ready cache invalidation
commits.body:
Follow-up sweep on 2932b79 covering 9 findings from the review pass:

cssColor.ts
- normalizeColor now returns string | null. null signals rejection so
  getCSSColor can route a present-but-rejected CSS variable to its hex
  fallback rather than re-feeding the unparseable value to Cytoscape
  (which would silently drop the style again).
- Sentinel comparison via regex (SENTINEL_RE) tolerates whitespace variants
  — historical WebKit serializes rgba without inner spaces.
- getCtx() no longer marks itself initialized on failure. A first call
  before DOM-ready or in a hardened env no longer locks the module into
  a null ctx for its lifetime.
- try/catch around both fillStyle assignments. Trusted Types / sandbox
  throw is treated as rejection instead of crashing the render path.
- Each rejected raw value logs once via debugLog('canvas', 'warn', ...).
  Set-based dedupe so repeated calls don't flood. clearCSSColorCache()
  resets both the value cache and the dedupe set.

config.ts + GraphCanvas.tsx
- wheelSensitivity exposed as config.wheelSensitivity (default 0.3).
  Cytoscape's library default (1.0) is widely regarded as too aggressive
  on desktop mice; 0.3 is the common override. The one-time Cytoscape
  info warning is documented and expected. Deployments that prefer
  library default set window.__GRAPHIKI_CONFIG__ = { wheelSensitivity: 1.0 }.

GraphCanvas.tsx
- document.fonts.ready effect invalidates cssColor cache and bumps a
  stylesReady flag so the style-apply effect re-runs with fresh CSS
  reads. Fixes the "Cytoscape inits before DaisyUI stylesheet paints"
  race where hex fallbacks got cached under the real variable names
  and later theme-reapply effects hit stale cache.

debugLog.ts
- MAX_ENTRIES 200 → 500. The original oklch flood emitted 28 warnings
  on init alone, erasing boot/render context under the tight cap.
  Memory cost ~100 KB.

pwaDiagnostics.ts
- New "Graph Style Rejections" check scans the debug buffer for canvas-
  normalizer rejections and intercepted Cytoscape "style property is
  invalid" warns. Green when zero, red with count when non-zero —
  surfaces the class of bug as a diagnostic instead of burying it in
  console noise.

cssColor.test.ts
- 12 → 18 tests. Switched to vi.spyOn + vi.restoreAllMocks pattern so a
  mid-test throw can't poison sibling files. New cases: null-on-rejection,
  whitespace variants in sentinel serialization, fillStyle throw, log-
  once dedupe, fallback-on-rejection regression guard, fallback-when-
  both-reject defense, dedupe-set invalidation. Known-limitation test
  documents that non-transparent zero-alpha forms are misclassified as
  rejection (harmless — null routes to caller's hex fallback; DaisyUI
  tokens are all opaque).

Docs
- CLAUDE.md: cssColor and wheelSensitivity bullets updated for new
  behavior (null-on-rejection, fonts.ready invalidation, config gate).
- docs/CODEBASE_DOCUMENTATION.md: Cytoscape Theme Awareness section
  expanded with null-return semantics, fonts.ready race fix, test
  infrastructure, known limitation.
- docs/AI_MISTAKES.md: wheelSensitivity entry reframed — a library
  warning doesn't always mean the default is correct; the right fix
  was exposing the setting via config, not silencing by reverting.
- docs/SESSION_NOTES.md: refreshed summary.
- docs/TODO.md: worker-side debug logging + central error-logging layer
  added as tech-debt items (deferred from this PR).

Deferred out of this PR (tracked in TODO.md):
- Worker-side console interception (embeddingWorker, tagSuggestionWorker
  emit warns that never reach DebugPill).
- Central error-logging layer wrapping the ~20 catch { showToast(...) }
  blocks that swallow underlying errors.

commits.subject: fix(graph): normalize oklch() CSS variables before handing them to Cytoscape
commits.body:
DaisyUI v5 emits oklch() values for its semantic color tokens. Cytoscape's
style engine only accepts hex/rgb/named colors, so every themed property
backed by --color-warning / --color-info / --color-success / --color-secondary
/ --color-accent / --color-primary / --color-base-content was silently
rejected — selected-node borders, dual-query A/B/bridge/overlap colors,
analysis highlights, and arc fallback colors reverted to Cytoscape defaults
on every install that rendered the app in a modern browser. Only symptom
in logs was a flood of "The style property ... is invalid" warnings.

cssColor.ts now routes raw computed values through a shared 2D canvas's
fillStyle, which the HTML spec forces to serialize any valid CSS color to
#rrggbb / rgba(...) / #rrggbbaa. Results cached by raw input. A transparent-
black sentinel written before the real assignment detects inputs the browser
silently rejects so they stay un-cached and a later theme load can retry.
getCSSColor's signature is unchanged; all 7 callsites work as-is.

Also removes the custom wheelSensitivity: 0.3 (Cytoscape author's own
advice: "change only if you can guarantee all users will use the same
hardware" — a guarantee a cross-device PWA can't make). Kills the one-time
warning without trading it for unpredictable zoom feel across mice,
trackpads, and touchscreens.

Tests: cssColor.test.ts (12) covers normalization, hex pass-through, cache,
sentinel detection, non-caching of rejections, transparent-vs-sentinel
disambiguation, missing-canvas fallback, empty-input short-circuit, CSS
variable read + fallback, and cache invalidation. Full suite: 302/302.

Docs: CLAUDE.md (cssColor entry, Cytoscape normalization + wheelSensitivity
conventions), CODEBASE_DOCUMENTATION.md (expanded Cytoscape Theme Awareness),
AI_MISTAKES.md (new class-of-bug entry), SESSION_NOTES.md, TODO.md (follow-up
dev-only assertion on Cytoscape warnings).

commits.subject: fix(ui): restore mobile column layout for toolbar, DaisyUI btn classes
commits.body:
Root cause of double-tap regression: commit 08a80bb migrated .app-toolbar
from custom CSS to Tailwind utilities but dropped flex-direction: column
on mobile. Original CSS:
  @media (max-width: 768px) { .app-toolbar { flex-direction: column; } }
Tailwind replacement was flex row on all sizes, causing ViewSwitcher
(shrink-0 max-md:w-full) to consume 100% row width and push QueryBuilder
to 0 width. The QueryBuilder's invisible overflow sat on top of the tabs,
consuming the first tap.

Fix: add max-md:flex-col max-md:gap-2 max-md:px-3 to toolbar container,
matching the original column layout on mobile.

Removed touch-manipulation band-aid from ViewSwitcher — it was masking
the layout bug, not fixing it.

- CreateNodeModal/ConnectNodeModal: custom cancel buttons → btn btn-ghost
- ConnectNodeModal: custom list item buttons → btn btn-ghost btn-sm

commits.subject: feat(labels): add zero-shot NLI label suggestion system
commits.body:
Implement label suggestions using zero-shot NLI classification
(xtremedistil-l6-h256-zeroshot-v1.1-all-33, ~13MB q8 model) that
suggests relevant labels when creating or editing nodes. The system
uses a separate Web Worker from the embedding engine since it runs
a different model and pipeline type.

Architecture:
- tagSuggestionWorker.ts: Web Worker for NLI classification
- tagSuggestionService.ts: Worker lifecycle with ref-counted disposal
- useTagSuggestions.ts: React hook with acquire/release lifecycle
- LabelSuggestions.tsx: Inline suggestion badges below label inputs

commits.subject: fix(merge): consistent normalization + workspace-only node arc resolution
commits.body:
Two bugs fixed in getApprovedItems:

1. Normalization inconsistency: getApprovedItems used inline
   .trim().toLowerCase() while buildMergeProposal used normalizeTitle()
   (which also collapses internal whitespace). Titles like "Alice  Chen"
   would normalize differently between the two functions, causing arc
   endpoint resolution to silently fail.

   arc endpoints against import proposal nodes. Arcs referencing nodes
   that exist in the workspace but weren't part of the import file were
   silently dropped. Now accepts optional existingNodes parameter with
   the same two-layer resolution pattern as buildMergeProposal —
   workspace nodes as fallback, proposal results as authoritative layer.

- Whitespace normalization consistency between functions
- Workspace-only node arc resolution via existingNodes parameter

  layout, sortBy, filters, rendering, timeProperty) and orphaned
  FilterConfig interface.

- Add safeStorage.ts utility with safeGetItem/safeSetItem/safeRemoveItem
  for localStorage in restricted environments (Safari private browsing).
  Applied to useWorkspaces, App.tsx, TutorialModal, seedService.

- Update all affected tests and documentation.

commits.subject: fix(pwa): capture beforeinstallprompt before React mounts to prevent race condition
commits.body:
The beforeinstallprompt event can fire as soon as the service worker
registers (cached on repeat visits), which may happen before React's
useEffect attaches its listener — causing the install prompt to never
appear on Chromium browsers.

Fix: Add an inline classic script in index.html that captures the event
immediately during HTML parse. The usePWAInstall hook's state initializer
consumes the pre-captured event synchronously on first render. A fallback
addEventListener covers first-visit cases where the SW registers after
React mounts. Analytics tracking is centralized in a single useEffect
to avoid both the race condition and React lint violations.

Tags: pwa, install-prompt, race-condition, beforeinstallprompt

commits.subject: fix(db): restore version(1) and add v4 migration for sourceId→source rename
commits.body:
The v3 migration (commit 64f99ce) failed to migrate arc data from
sourceId/targetId to source/target for two reasons:

1. The version(1) declaration was removed in commit 65bdc4c, breaking
   the Dexie upgrade chain for users whose IndexedDB was created at v1.
   Without the v1 schema definition, Dexie cannot properly compute the
   upgrade path.

2. The v3 upgrade condition `!('source' in arc)` fails when Dexie adds
   an undefined `source` key during the v2 index rename — the `in`
   operator returns true even when the value is undefined, causing the
   migration to silently skip every arc.

Fixes:
- Restores the version(1) declaration with the original sourceId/targetId
  indexed schema so the full v1→v2→v3→v4 upgrade chain works.
- Adds version(4) with a robust migration using value-based checks
  (`!arc.source`) instead of key-existence checks. This catches all
  users — including those already at v3 whose data was never migrated.

Symptom: on mobile (Android Chrome 145), all 44 demo arcs logged
"Skipping arc — missing source or target" and the graph rendered
with nodes only, no edges.

Tags: db, migration, dexie, mobile, bug

objects. Users who visited before that rename have arcs in IndexedDB with
sourceId/targetId properties — arc.source is undefined, crashing

in GraphCanvas to skip arcs with falsy or dangling source/target
references, logging skipped arcs via debugLog so the debug pill
surfaces the issue rather than silently swallowing it.

commits.body:

commits.subject: fix(import): handle multiple existing nodes with same title in merge
commits.body:
The merge service used Map<string, GraphNode> for title-based matching,
which silently dropped all but the last existing node when multiple shared
a title (e.g., "Apple" [Company] vs "Apple" [Fruit]). Imported "Apple"
would match whichever was stored last, ignoring labels entirely.

Now uses Map<string, GraphNode[]> and ranks candidates by label overlap:
exact label match first, then most shared labels, then first candidate
as fallback. Each imported node independently picks its best match.

commits.subject: fix(import): normalize JSON import to handle AI output guide format
commits.body:
The JSON import was blindly casting raw data as GraphNode[]/Arc[] without
transformation. When AI assistants followed the output guide (which uses
`tags`, `typeName`, `isSystemNode`, `schemas`, version "1"), the data
was silently malformed — `tags` were lost, `typeName` ignored, and
required fields like `labels`, `viewData`, `properties` left undefined.

Added normalization layer in validateJsonImport that:
- Maps `tags` → `labels` (AI guide uses `tags`, internal model uses `labels`)
- Maps `typeName` → appends to `labels` (type classification as label)
- Merges `labels` + `tags` + `typeName` with deduplication
- Defaults missing fields: labels=[], properties={}, viewData={},
  content='', contentType='text/markdown', timestamps=now
- Strips legacy fields (isSystemNode, isSystemArc, schemas)
- Accepts both version "1" (guide format) and "2" (native export)

Markdown and CSV imports were already correct — only JSON path was broken.

Tags: import, json, normalization, ai-guide, compatibility

commits.subject: feat(conventions): add strict naming and consistency rules for imports
commits.body:
Extracted a shared NAMING_CONVENTIONS block appended to both JSON and
Markdown convention content. The rules are prescriptive enough that two
independent AI sessions given the same real-world data should produce
identical titles, labels, arc titles, and property keys — enabling
reliable duplicate detection by the merge service.

Key additions:
- Title rules: most widely recognized name, no legal suffixes, no
  honorifics, no abbreviations unless the acronym IS the common name
- Label rules: singular PascalCase, standard vocabulary of 19 common
  labels, broad-over-narrow preference
- Arc title rules: lowercase verb phrases, active voice, present/past
  tense distinction, standard vocabulary of 26 common relationships
- Property rules: camelCase keys, standard vocabulary, typed values
  (real numbers, booleans, ISO dates, arrays instead of CSV strings)
- Deduplication guidance: reuse IDs for same entity, merge aliases,
  no duplicate arcs

commits.subject: fix(ui): detail drawer opens on click, timeline dots aligned on line
commits.body:
Two bugs fixed:

1. Detail drawer never opened when clicking a node or arc. Root cause:
   cy.on('tap', handleTapBackground) fires on ALL taps, including
   bubbled events from nodes and edges. The background handler cleared
   the selection immediately after the node handler set it. Fix: check
   evt.target === cy before clearing selection.

2. Timeline marker dots were positioned ~15px to the right of the
   vertical track line. Root cause: negative margin math was incorrect
   under box-sizing: border-box (margin-left: -22px produced a dot
   center at 24px, but the line center is at 9px). Fix: switch to
   absolute positioning (left: -25px) for precise geometric alignment.

Tags: ui, graph-canvas, timeline, event-handling, css

Design fixes:
- deleteNode now emits arc:deleted for each cascade-deleted arc instead
  of silently bulk-deleting. Collects arc IDs first, then deletes and
  emits per-arc events so arc-specific listeners stay consistent.
- loadAllData now filters system nodes/arcs via getAllNodes/getAllArcs
  instead of raw db.toArray(), preventing system items from leaking
  into the graph canvas and views.
- getAllNodes/getAllArcs changed from .where().equals(0) to .filter()
  for boolean isSystem fields — .equals(0) fails in fake-indexeddb
  which preserves JS booleans instead of coercing to 0/1.
- Analysis panel source/target validated against current filtered nodes
  via derived validSourceId/validTargetId. Stale selections (from nodes
  removed by query filter) are treated as empty, disabling Run button.
- Config changed from eager module-load evaluation to lazy Proxy-based
  access. window.__GRAPHIKI_CONFIG__ is now read on first property access,
  supporting override scripts loaded after the app bundle.

commits.subject: feat(analysis): add Phase 5 graph analysis algorithms + visualization
commits.body:
Implements six graph algorithms as pure functions on adjacency lists,
with a collapsible UI panel for running them and visual overlays on
the graph canvas for displaying results.

Algorithms implemented:
- Degree centrality: identifies hub nodes by connection count
- Betweenness centrality (Brandes): finds bridge nodes between clusters
- PageRank (power iteration): ranks nodes by network influence
- Connected components (BFS): partitions graph into disconnected groups
- Shortest path (BFS): finds shortest route between two selected nodes
- Clustering coefficient: measures local triangle density

Visualization:
- Score-based node sizing (30-70px scaled by normalized score)
- Path highlighting with accent color on nodes and arcs
- Component coloring with 10-color palette
- Non-highlighted elements dimmed to 20% opacity
- Cytoscape class-based styling (analysis-dimmed, analysis-highlighted)

UI:
- AnalysisPanel with algorithm dropdown, description, source/target pickers
- Top-20 results table sorted by score
- Path display with arrow notation

commits.subject: feat(schema): add Phase 2 schema layer — types, builder UI, and schema-aware creation
commits.body:
Implements the full schema layer for Graphiki, enabling structured graph
data through user-defined schemas with node types, arc types, connection
rules, and property definitions.

Service layer:
- schemaService.ts: Schema CRUD, type lookups, connection validation
- factory.ts: Factories for schemas, node types, arc types, properties

Hooks:
- useAppMode: User/designer mode toggle with localStorage persistence
- useSchemaData: Live-updating schema list with event subscription

Schema Builder UI (designer mode):
- SchemaList: Create, edit, delete, activate schemas
- SchemaEditor: Tabbed editor for details, types, and rules
- NodeTypeEditor/ArcTypeEditor: Name, color, properties editing
- PropertyDefEditor: Dynamic property type configuration
- ConnectionRuleEditor: Define valid arc-to-node connections

Schema-aware data creation:
- CreateNodeModal: Type selector + dynamic property form
- ConnectModal: Arc type dropdown with connection rule validation (permissive)

Graph visualization:
- GraphCanvas: Type-based node colors and arc styles via Cytoscape data attributes
- DetailPanel: Type badges, schema-defined property display and editing

App shell:
- Mode toggle button (User/Designer) in header
- Active schema badge display
- Designer mode shows Schema Builder in side panel

Also adds delete confirmation dialogs for destructive actions and
fixes React 19 lint compliance (key-based remounting over useEffect setState).
